intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: Registry reconciliation indicates significant divergence from the baseline. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/unidentifiable. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not clearly distinguished in the shared black pot. P3 (Pothos) is present with the anchor.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: Yellow pot appears empty/soil only.
inventory_reconciliation.P2_P4: Ambiguous: Shared black pot contains a single, struggling specimen.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: Pothos leaf and white rabbit anchor confirmed.
inventory_reconciliation.New_Interventions: Introduction of eggshell fragments in the black pot (likely calcium supplementation) and an electronic component (IC/chip) on the desk surface.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no visible growth.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Single, chlorotic, thin-stemmed specimen showing signs of severe etiolation.
plant_audit.P3: Single leaf remaining; appears turgid but isolated.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots; no signs of surface cracking.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments in the black pot; electronic component (IC) on the desk surface near the white paper.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Stable; minimal change in specimen posture.
temporal_deltas.T-2: Low light/exposure event; specimen in black pot appears slightly more collapsed.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_Current: Recovery of posture in the black pot specimen; eggshell fragments remain static.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of 'Stasis/Survival'. P3 is the only stable specimen. The shared pot (P2/P4) is suffering from severe light deficiency, evidenced by the thin, elongated stem. The lack of growth in P1 suggests a potential dormancy or total loss.
anomalies: Presence of an integrated circuit (IC) on the desk surface.
Deliberate placement of eggshell fragments in the soil of the black pot.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is currently under-resourced. The Pothos (P3) remains the most viable occupant. The shared black pot contains a single, struggling plant that is likely not the intended Mexican Mint or Silver Guest, or is a severely etiolated remnant of one. The yellow pot (P1) is currently a non-productive soil medium. The introduction of eggshells suggests an attempt at soil amendment, while the presence of electronic debris indicates a non-sterile, multi-use desk environment.
